description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a roughly triangular profile and a slightly pointed end. Its surface is predominantly a pale beige with patches of reddish-brown and darker stains, suggesting natural mineral deposits or weathering. The stone has a rugged, uneven texture with visible indentations, creases, and some smoother areas, especially on the broader face. One side features a hand-written marking, possibly for cataloging or identification, indicating it may have been collected or studied. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one edge more sharply curved and the opposite side flatter. The stone appears dense and solid, typical of sedimentary or weathered urban stones found in parks or roadside environments.
description: A rugged, beige stone with reddish-brown patches, irregular shape, and a hand-written marking on one side.
description_view_0: The broadest face of the stone is visible, showing a rugged, uneven surface with pale and reddish-brown coloration.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the stone's thickness and a pronounced curve, highlighting its asymmetrical profile.
description_view_2: The flattest face is shown, featuring a hand-written marking and a smoother, more uniform surface coloration.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, similar to view 1, but with a slightly different curvature and visible surface creases.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as an obstacle barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with patches of reddish-brown and dark stains; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and uneven with visible discoloration, indicating moderate natural weathering and exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban environments, may partially blend with natural landscaping or be used as a visual barrier.
